title: iEEG_comprehensive_HFA_model_part1
description: This dataset comprises intracranial EEG recordings acquired during non-REM sleep from multiple subjects. The data were collected to develop and validate a comprehensive model of high-frequency activity (HFA) in epilepsy research. This represents part 1 of a multi-part dataset designed to characterize neural oscillations and their clinical significance during sleep states. This dataset is a curated version of OpenNeuro dataset ds007118 (v1.0.0), with additional enrichment and standardization for the NEMAR platform.

methods_description: Intracranial EEG data were recorded during non-REM sleep. Recordings were conducted at multiple clinical sites including Wayne State University, Tohoku University, and the National Center of Neurology and Psychiatry, with ethics approval from institutional review boards at each location.
